events.text: Barbara Nguyen, 67-year-old White female, Former smoker (quit 10 years ago). Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.

Diagnosed with Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3 of the Right kidney. Clinical staging: T3bN0M1, AJCC Stage IV. ICD-10: C64.9. Stage IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma with lung metastases.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.
Underwent cytoreductive nephrectomy: Right radical nephrectomy with adrenalectomy. Estimated blood loss 400 mL. Length of stay 5 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.
Initiated first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w + Axitinib 5 mg BID. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w + Axitinib 5 mg BID. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Developed grade 1 proteinuria. 24-hour urine protein 1.2 g. Monitoring continued. No dose modification needed.
Initiated first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w + Axitinib 5 mg BID. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 hand-foot syndrome. Urea-based emollient prescribed. Dose reduction per protocol.
Initiated first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w + Axitinib 5 mg BID. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging after first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination.
Clinical visit for results review after first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination.
Initiated second-line immunotherapy-TKI: Nivolumab 240 mg q2w + Cabozantinib 40 mg daily. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for follow-up on second-line immunotherapy-TKI.
Clinical visit for follow-up on second-line immunotherapy-TKI.
MRI abdomen with contrast performed for restaging on second-line immunotherapy-TKI.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Bone scan performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Bone scan performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.

documents.text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Barbara Nguyen
MRN: patient_cdca513a2221
Date of Study: 2022-01-13

CLINICAL INDICATION: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Solid enhancing mass arising from the right kidney measuring 6.3 x 5.0 cm with extension into the renal vein.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Right renal mass consistent with renal cell carcinoma. Pulmonary metastases.

Electronically signed by: David Kim, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Barbara Nguyen
MRN: patient_cdca513a2221
Specimen ID: S2022-88679
Date of Procedure: 2022-01-15
Date of Report: 2022-01-17

SPECIMEN: Right kidney, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 6.2 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 1.2 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Present

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PAX8: Positive
CA-IX: Positive (diffuse membranous)
CD10: Positive
CK7: Negative
Vimentin: Positive

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Barbara Nguyen - Right kidney" is a tan-pink tissue fragment measuring 6.2 x 4.3 x 3.1 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A8.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of cords and trabeculae of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows chronic inflammation.

Electronically signed by: James Park, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Urology

Patient: Barbara Nguyen
MRN: patient_cdca513a2221
Date: 2022-01-20
Provider: Dr. Helen Park

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Nguyen is a 67-year-old female who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed clear cell renal cell carcinoma, fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney, IV. Patient was referred by primary care physician after abnormal imaging. Stage IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma with lung metastases. Pathology confirmed clear cell renal cell carcinoma, fuhrman grade 3. Molecular testing has been sent. Patient reports fatigue and unintentional 10-lb weight loss over 3 months.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- coronary artery disease
- obstructive sleep apnea
- osteoarthritis

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Cesarean section x 2 (if applicable)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Former smoker (quit 10 years ago)
- Alcohol: No alcohol use
- Occupation: Engineer
- Lives with alone

FAMILY HISTORY:
- No family history of cancer.

ALLERGIES: Latex (contact dermatitis)

MEDICATIONS:
- None

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: dry mouth, unintentional weight loss, mild peripheral edema. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 123/70, HR 83, RR 17, Temp 98.8F, SpO2 95% on RA, Wt 62.7 kg
General: Comfortable, thin-appearing, no acute distress.
HEENT: NCAT, EOMI, PERRL. Oral mucosa moist without lesions.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear bilaterally. No rales or rhonchi.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, mildly tender in RUQ. No rebound. Normoactive bowel sounds.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. Good pulses bilaterally.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 6.4, Hgb 12.1, Plt 243, Cr 0.88

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
67-year-old female with IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma, fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney. initial oncology consultation.

1. Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney, Stage IV
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend cytoreductive nephrectomy
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - Peripheral access adequate
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Priya Sharma, MD
Urology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Barbara Nguyen
MRN: patient_cdca513a2221
Date of Report: 2022-01-31
Specimen: Right kidney (Resection)
Diagnosis: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3
Panel: Tempus xT (523 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 3.5 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  VHL p.L89P (c.266T>C) - Missense (Exon 1) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  PBRM1 p.Q1298* - Nonsense (Exon 24)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  KDM5C p.A388T - VUS
  MTOR p.S2215Y - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - VHL mutation: Consistent with clear cell histology. HIF-pathway targeted therapies (TKIs) and immunotherapy combinations recommended.
  - BAP1 loss: Associated with more aggressive biology.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 684x coverage.
Tumor content: 59%.

Electronically signed by: Maria Santos, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Barbara Nguyen
MRN: patient_cdca513a2221
Specimen ID: S2022-48672
Date of Procedure: 2022-02-13
Date of Report: 2022-02-15

SPECIMEN: Right kidney, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3, moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 7.9 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PAX8: Positive
CA-IX: Positive (diffuse membranous)
CD10: Positive
CK7: Negative
Vimentin: Positive

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Barbara Nguyen - Right kidney" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 7.9 x 5.5 x 4.0 cm. The cut surface reveals a soft white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A8.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows minimal reactive changes.

Electronically signed by: Patricia Williams, MD
Department of Pathology

FOLLOW-UP NOTE - Medical Oncology

MRN: patient_cdca513a2221

Provider: Dr. Richard Okafor

CHIEF COMPLAINT: postoperative follow-up

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Nguyen is a 67-year-old female with IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma, fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney who presents for postoperative follow-up. Patient reports mild fatigue but otherwise doing well. Weight is stable.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: unintentional weight loss, intermittent nausea, insomnia. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 134/85, HR 78, RR 18, Temp 97.6F, SpO2 97% on RA, Wt 77.9 kg
General: Well-nourished, alert, in no distress.
HEENT: No scleral icterus. Oropharynx clear.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear bilaterally. No rales or rhonchi.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-distended. Well-healed midline surgical scar.
Extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema. Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 8.9, Hgb 12.3, Plt 213, Cr 0.83

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
67-year-old female with IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma, fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney. postoperative follow-up.

1. Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3 - postoperative follow-up
   - Continue current treatment regimen
   - Labs today show adequate counts for treatment
2. Symptom management
   - Fatigue management discussed
3. Follow-up in 6 weeks

Electronically signed by: Amanda Foster, MD

Patient: Barbara Nguyen
MRN: patient_cdca513a2221
Date of Study: 2022-12-11

CLINICAL INDICATION: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Comparison made to prior study from approximately 3 months ago.

TECHNIQUE: Whole body bone scintigraphy performed following IV injection of 25 mCi Tc-99m MDP.

FINDINGS:
No scintigraphic evidence of osseous metastatic disease. Degenerative uptake in bilateral knees.

IMPRESSION:
1. Right renal mass consistent with renal cell carcinoma. Pulmonary metastases.
2. Overall assessment: Partial response per RECIST 1.1.
